sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Mixpanel connector integrates your Mixpanel project with Shielva by streaming raw events via the Data Export API (NDJSON) and querying analytics data (segmentation, funnels, retention) via the Mixpanel API. Authentication uses HTTP Basic Auth with a Mixpanel Service Account (username + secret). Both US and EU data-residency regions are supported. Each raw event is normalized into a ConnectorDocument with a stable 16-char SHA-256-based id for idempotent syncs.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content: HTTP Basic Auth: Authorization: Basic base64(username:secret). username = Mixpanel Service Account Username. secret = Mixpanel Service Account Secret. Service accounts are created in Project Settings → Service Accounts and require at least Analyst (read) role. No OAuth flow required.
steps: Log in to mixpanel.com and open your project.
Navigate to Settings → Project Settings to find your Project ID.
Go to Settings → Project Settings → Service Accounts.
Click Add Service Account, name it, assign Analyst role.
Copy the Service Account Username and Secret shown at creation (secret shown only once).
Enter username, secret, project_id (and optionally region) in Shielva.

fields.label: Service Account Username
fields.description: Mixpanel Service Account Username. Created in Project Settings → Service Accounts.

fields.label: Service Account Secret
fields.description: Mixpanel Service Account Secret. Generated at creation — shown only once.

fields.label: Project ID
fields.description: Mixpanel Project ID. Found in Project Settings → Project ID.

fields.label: Region (US or EU)
fields.description: Data residency region. Set to EU for EU data residency projects. Defaults to US.

methods.name: get_projects
methods.description: Returns current user and accessible projects. Used for health check and install validation.

methods.name: query_events
methods.signature: async def query_events(event_names=None, from_date=None, to_date=None, limit=100) -> AsyncGenerator
methods.description: NDJSON export of raw events. Returns async generator yielding one dict per event line. Splits response by newlines and json.loads each line.
methods.note: Returns NDJSON — not a regular JSON array.

methods.name: query_funnels
methods.signature: async def query_funnels(funnel_id, from_date, to_date, unit='day') -> dict
methods.description: Funnel conversion data for a specific funnel_id.

methods.name: list_funnels
methods.description: Lists all saved funnels in the project.

methods.name: query_retention
methods.signature: async def query_retention(from_date, to_date, retention_type='birth', interval=1, unit='week') -> dict

methods.name: query_segmentation
methods.signature: async def query_segmentation(event, from_date, to_date, type='general', unit='day') -> dict
methods.description: Aggregated event counts by date segment.

methods.name: get_event_properties
methods.description: Property metadata for a named event.

methods.name: install
methods.description: Validates username, secret, project_id presence then calls get_projects(). Returns HEALTHY/CONNECTED on success.

methods.name: health_check
methods.description: Calls get_projects() and returns current health status.

methods.name: sync
methods.description: Queries events for the last 30 days, normalizes each to a ConnectorDocument, returns SyncResult.

methods.name: query_events
methods.signature: async def query_events(event_names=None, from_date=None, to_date=None, limit=100) -> list[dict]
methods.description: Returns list of raw event dicts from the NDJSON export endpoint.

methods.name: query_funnel
methods.signature: async def query_funnel(funnel_id, from_date=None, to_date=None) -> dict
methods.description: Returns funnel data dict. Uses last 30 days if dates not provided.

methods.name: query_segmentation
methods.signature: async def query_segmentation(event, from_date=None, to_date=None) -> dict
methods.description: Returns segmentation data dict. Uses last 30 days if dates not provided.

fields.name: content
fields.description: Newline-delimited key-value of all event properties

exceptions.name: MixpanelError
exceptions.description: Base exception. Carries message, status_code, code.
